arts.ttl: How to watch Arsenal vs Newcastle FOR FREE: TV channel and live stream for Carabao Cup today
arts.sum: First leg of huge semi-final as both teams dream of Wembley

arts.ttl: Newcastle United squad revealed for Arsenal with Bruno replacement and captain decision
arts.sum: Newcastle United head to Arsenal for tonight's crucial Carabao Cup semi-final first leg without six first-team players, but with a strong squad confident of bringing a positive result back to Tyneside for next month's second leg. Sven Botman made a welcome return the first team against Tottenham on Saturday and will keep his place in the squad, although Howe will liaise with medical staff over whether the Dutch defender is able to play two games in such a short space of time after a nine-month lay-off.

arts.ttl: Revealed: Arsenal ready to keep pursuing attacking duo regardless of new contracts
arts.sum: Arsenal could still revisit their interest in two of their top forward targets in the summer transfer window, regardless of new contracts. The Gunners remain keen on RB Leipzig striker Benjamin Sesko even though he signed a new contract last year, and CaughtOffside understands he’ll likely be one of their top targets again for summer […] The post Revealed: Arsenal ready to keep pursuing attacking duo regardless of new contracts appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: How to watch Arsenal vs Newcastle for FREE: TV and streaming info for Carabao Cup tie
arts.sum: Arsenal and Newcastle United will meet in the first leg of their Carabao Cup semi final tonight as Mikel Arteta and Eddie Howe vie for a spot in the final at Wembley

arts.ttl: Arsenal vs Newcastle United TV channel, live stream, kick off time
arts.sum: United head to North London full of confidence, and with three away wins on the bounce under their belts, but know Arsenal will be a tough nut to crack tonight. Anthony Gordon is expected to be fit after taking a blow to the face against Spurs, while Howe must decide whether Sven Botman can play two games in four days after his nine-month absence.

arts.ttl: Arsenal Bryan Mbeumo transfer message after Mikel Arteta's injury crisis worsens
arts.sum: So, with that in mind, football.london has taken a look at the latest news around Arsenal in the January transfer window. football.london says: Arsenal have already been linked with Cunha and on current form that is understandable, but if they want an impact signing Mbeumo is the man to go for.

The Spaniard's tunnel vision for the league has previously hindered the Gunners in the cups, but Liverpool's superiority at the summit means Arsenal will likely have to search elsewhere to claim silverware in year five of Arteta's project. That victory over Crystal Palace inspired a three-game winning run, but Arsenal head into Tuesday's first leg with Newcastle off the back of a 1-1 draw with Brighton .

arts.ttl: Report: Deal agreed for Newcastle star; move expected to be completed after Arsenal game
arts.sum: Newcastle United are on the verge of bidding farewell to veteran goalkeeper Martin Dubravka as the club looks to refine its squad during the January transfer window. The 35-year-old, who has served as a reliable backup to first-choice keeper Nick Pope, is set to join Saudi Arabian club Al-Shabab. arts.ttl: Carabao Cup semi-finals to see major VAR change as Mikel Arteta makes feelings clear
arts.sum: Arsenal welcome Newcastle and Tottenham host Liverpool in the first legs of the Carabao Cup semi-finals this week, and fans in the stadium and viewers at home are set to see a change to VAR

arts.ttl: Supercomputer predicts final Premier League table after Liverpool & Arsenal slip-ups
arts.sum: Opta's supercomputer predicts the final 2024/25 Premier League table after the title hopefuls drop points on Gameweek 20.
